name: e2e-artifact-deduplication
trigger_phrases: Use this skill when you observe duplicate files across judge runs, large evaluation directories, inlined content in scripts, per-judge artifacts for shared data, or symlinks to mutable sources.

name: e2e-batch-result-analysis
trigger_phrases: (1) A batch E2E run has completed and `batch_summary.json` exists; (2) You need to triage which failures are framework bugs vs model limitations.

name: e2e-claude-cli-pipeline-oom-fixes
trigger_phrases: Use when `claude-myrmidon.py` (or similar multi-stage NATS pipeline) OOM-kills a 16GB WSL2 system, or each `claude -p` invocation consumes ~500MB-1GB RAM.

name: file-upstream-feature-request
trigger_phrases: About to file a feature request or bug against an upstream OSS tool you don't maintain; want to include a proposed patch as supporting evidence (not a PR — just a reference).

name: fixme-todo-cleanup
trigger_phrases: User requests cleanup of FIXME/TODO items across a codebase; technical debt reduction initiative; pre-release code quality sweep.

name: fixture-timeout-calibration
trigger_phrases: Trigger this skill when batch E2E runs finish but many tests use generic default timeout values that are far too conservative.

name: flaky-test-patch-isolation
trigger_phrases: Apply this pattern when a test is flaky only in the full suite (passes in isolation or smaller subsets), the test suite has classes with `patch.object(SomeClass, "method", ...)`, or flakiness correlates with test ordering.

name: flesh-out-scaffolded-repo
trigger_phrases: Use when a repo was scaffolded and committed but lacks a `justfile` / `pixi.toml` for task running, compose files or Nomad HCL have hardcoded absolute paths, or shell scripts use `|| true` to suppress failures.

name: forbid-e2e-remote-ops
trigger_phrases: Use this skill when E2E test agents are pushing to remote repositories or creating real PRs during test runs, or you need to enforce test environment isolation.

name: fp16-accumulation-threshold-math-docs
trigger_phrases: Use when a GitHub issue is a follow-up to an implementation issue that omitted a Float16 limitations section from the Success Criteria, or a `docs/dev/testing-strategy.md` Gradient Checking section needs a subsection explaining why convolution tests skip Float16.

name: fp16-precision-test-documentation
headings_h2: ## Key Technical Facts for Float16 Precision Documentation
trigger_phrases: Use when a GitHub issue requests reviewing and documenting Float16 precision NOTEs in test files, or test files have scattered inline `# NOTE: Float16 precision insufficient...` comments.

name: frontmatter-colon-split-fix
trigger_phrases: Use when a frontmatter parser reads YAML values using `line.split(":", 1)` or `partition(":")`, or description fields are being silently truncated when they contain URLs or colons.

name: generate-api-docs
trigger_phrases: Use when documenting public module interfaces, creating reference guides for libraries, generating HTML API documentation, or maintaining up-to-date API specs.

name: generate-boilerplate
trigger_phrases: Use when creating new modules or packages, setting up test file structure, initializing configuration files, or standardizing code structure across project.

name: generate-changelog
trigger_phrases: Use when writing `git log --pretty=format` strings that split output into fields, parsing conventional commit messages (`type(scope): message`), or extracting scope from commit prefixes that may contain nested parentheses.

name: generate-docstrings
trigger_phrases: Use when adding documentation to undocumented functions, improving code documentation completeness, ensuring consistent docstring format, or supporting API documentation generation.

name: generate-fix-suggestions
trigger_phrases: (1) Test failures need concrete solutions; (2) Build errors require remediation steps; (3) Creating PR comments with suggestions.

name: generate-tests
trigger_phrases: Use when following test-driven development (TDD) approach, adding tests to increase coverage, testing edge cases and error conditions, or validating refactoring doesn't break functionality.

name: generator-template-placeholder-cleanup
headings_h2: ## Key Insight: Two Kinds of TODOs in Generator Scripts
trigger_phrases: Use when a GitHub cleanup issue asks to 'review TODOs' in generator scripts, or generator scripts contain `# TODO:` comments inside template string literals.

name: getting-started-doc-audit
trigger_phrases: Use when stub files contain placeholder text, a follow-up audit is needed after installation.md is written, or markdown linting fails on broken fenced code blocks.

name: gh-batch-merge-by-labels
trigger_phrases: (1) Multiple PRs are ready for merge with same status label; (2) End-of-sprint or release batch merges needed; (3) Automating merge of dependent chain of PRs.

name: gh-check-ci-status
trigger_phrases: (1) Verifying PR checks are passing before merge; (2) Investigating CI failures; (3) Monitoring long-running CI jobs; (4) Checking before pushing changes.

name: gh-create-pr-linked
trigger_phrases: Use when after completing implementation work, ready to submit changes for review, or needing to link PR to GitHub issue.

name: gh-fix-pr-feedback
trigger_phrases: Use when PR has open review comments requiring responses, ready to implement reviewer's requested changes, or PR is blocked on feedback.

name: gh-get-review-comments
trigger_phrases: Use when checking for unresolved review feedback, analyzing reviewer feedback before fixing, verifying all comments have been addressed, or getting comment IDs for replies.

name: gh-implement-issue
trigger_phrases: Use when starting work on a new issue, need structured workflow from branch to PR, want to follow best practices end-to-end, or working on assigned GitHub issue.

name: gh-post-issue-update
trigger_phrases: (1) Reporting implementation progress; (2) Documenting design decisions; (3) Posting completion summaries; (4) Sharing findings or blockers.

name: gh-pr-review-workflow
trigger_phrases: (1) Checking for unresolved review feedback; (2) Analyzing reviewer feedback before fixing; (3) Verifying all comments have been addressed; (4) Getting comment IDs for replies.

name: gh-read-issue-context
trigger_phrases: (1) Before starting implementation work; (2) When prior context is needed from earlier work; (3) Resuming work after a break.

name: gh-reply-review-comment
trigger_phrases: Use when responding to inline code review feedback, addressing specific review comments, confirming fixes have been implemented, or updating reviewers on progress.

name: gh-review-pr
trigger_phrases: Use when reviewing a PR before merge, evaluating code quality and standards, checking CI status and test coverage, or providing feedback to contributors.

name: git-commits-on-local-main-recovery
trigger_phrases: Use when `git branch --show-current` returns `main` after completing implementation work, or `git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD` shows commits that belong on a feature branch.

name: git-rebase-over-deletion
trigger_phrases: Trigger this skill when CI `Test` workflow on `main` starts failing after a deprecation-removal commit, the failure is an `ImportError` for symbols that should exist (not deprecated).

name: git-stash-pop-conflict-semantic-merge
trigger_phrases: Use when `git stash pop` produces merge conflicts and Safety Net blocks `git checkout --` (discard) or `git stash drop` (delete), or a stash from an old branch conflicts with heavily refactored HEAD.

name: git-worktree-cleanup-preservation-audit
trigger_phrases: Use when user says 'clean up stale branches/worktrees/stashes' AND asks to preserve work or 'err on the side of keeping', or `git cherry origin/main <branch>` shows `+` commits on a branch whose PR was MERGED.

name: git-worktree-management-patterns
trigger_phrases: Use when creating isolated git worktrees for parallel development on multiple issues, switching between worktrees without stashing, syncing feature branches with main, or cleaning up single or multiple stale worktrees after PRs merge.

name: gitattributes-setup
trigger_phrases: Use when repository supports multiple platforms (Linux, macOS, Windows), no `.gitattributes` exists at repo root, or pre-commit hooks handle line-endings but git-level normalization is missing.

name: github-actions-ci-patterns
trigger_phrases: Use when setting up GitHub Actions CI for Mojo or Python/pytest projects with pixi, or CI pipeline is slow due to broken pixi caching.

name: github-auto-merge-no-ci-runs
trigger_phrases: Use when PR has `mergeStateStatus: CLEAN` and auto-merge armed but hasn't merged after hours, or `gh pr view <n> --json statusCheckRollup` returns empty array `[]`.

name: github-branch-protection-org-standardize
trigger_phrases: Use when applying an org-wide branch protection baseline to multiple repos at once (free GitHub plan), enforcing required CI status checks via rulesets with `integration_id`.

name: github-bulk-issue-filing-rate-limit-recovery
trigger_phrases: Use when filing 40+ issues per repo, or 200+ issues across a session, using multiple retry agents that may overlap with previous filing runs.

name: github-comment-dedup-across-restarts
trigger_phrases: Use when a long-running pipeline worker posts `## Stage: <name>` comments to GitHub issues and uses an in-memory dict to track comment IDs for PATCH updates, or process restarts cause the in-memory `_comment_ids` dict to be empty.

name: github-issues-semantic-branch-rename-gh-edit-graphql-verify
trigger_phrases: Use when a repository default branch changed and existing issues still mention the old branch, or `gh search issues <term>` returns both true branch references and false positives.

name: github-ruleset-pr-blocked-diagnose-missing-check-requirements
trigger_phrases: Use when `gh pr view` reports mergeStateStatus as BLOCKED with no review comments, `gh pr checks` reports no checks on the branch, or repository rulesets require code quality or code scanning results that the repo is not configured to emit.

name: github-ssh-commit-signing-fix-unknown-key-verification
trigger_phrases: Use when a GitHub PR is blocked by a required signed-commit rule, or `gh api repos/<OWNER>/<REPO>/commits/<SHA> --jq '.commit.verification'` returns `reason: "unknown_key"` or `reason: "unsigned"`.

name: gitleaks-version-upgrade
trigger_phrases: Use when a GitHub issue requests upgrading Gitleaks to the latest stable release, dependabot or a security audit flags an outdated Gitleaks pin, or the existing SHA256 digest in `security.yml` no longer matches the binary.

name: global-semaphore-parallelism
trigger_phrases: Use this approach when you need to limit concurrent workers across multiple process pools, share concurrency limits across process boundaries, or fix per-tier to global parallelism.

name: graceful-signal-handling
trigger_phrases: Use this pattern when long-running processes need to handle Ctrl+C gracefully, work should be saved on interruption (checkpoints), or partial results are valuable even if process doesn't complete.

name: gradient-checking-test-setup-fixes
trigger_phrases: Use when CI 'Core Gradient' test group fails with gradient checking mismatches, batch norm backward gradient check shows analytical~0 vs numerical~0.09, or any gradient check exceeds tolerance when gradient magnitudes are large (>10).

name: grading-consolidation
trigger_phrases: Use this approach when you observe duplicate grading logic across multiple files (models, judges, evaluators), inconsistent grade thresholds between different parts of the system, or configurable grading scales that are never actually customized.

name: mojo-promote-constants-to-public-api
trigger_phrases: Use when a constant is defined as a local `alias` or `comptime` in module `X` but is needed by modules that shouldn't depend on `X`, or issue title contains 'export from public API'.

name: mojo-public-api-table-update
trigger_phrases: Use when a `shared/__init__.mojo` has a flat 'will be available once implementation completes' comment block that has grown stale after some symbols were actually implemented and exported.

name: mojo-python-interop-patterns-guide
trigger_phrases: Use when calling Python libraries (numpy, etc.) from Mojo, converting between `PythonObject` and Mojo types (`Int`, `String`, `Float64`), or building Python extension modules (`.so`) with `PythonModuleBuilder`.

name: mojo-python-interop-to-stdlib
trigger_phrases: Use when a Mojo function uses `Python.import_module("os")`, `Python.import_module("pathlib")`, or `Python.import_module("builtins")` for file listing or sorting.

name: mojo-retry-pattern-ci-validator
trigger_phrases: Use when adding a new mojo workflow file, auditing existing workflows for retry-loop compliance, or enforcing CI after mojo-jit-crash-retry fix.

name: mojo-run-epoch-with-batches
trigger_phrases: Use when a Mojo training utility (`run_epoch_with_batches` or similar) accepts a `DataLoader` and a `step_fn: fn(ExTensor, ExTensor) raises -> ExTensor` but has no tests.

name: mojo-runtime-crash-bisection
trigger_phrases: Use when Mojo program crashes with stack traces in `libKGENCompilerRTShared.so` or `libAsyncRTRuntimeGlobals.so`, crash output is just `execution crashed` with no helpful error message.

name: mojo-runtime-output-pattern-audit
trigger_phrases: Use when an audit issue requests follow-up coverage for `print('WARNING: ...')`, `print('HACK: ...')`, `print('XXX: ...')`, or placeholder messages like `Not implemented`.

name: mojo-sequential-parametric-containers
trigger_phrases: Use when composing two or more `Module`-conforming Mojo structs into an ordered forward chain, or seeing `ImplicitlyCopyable` errors when trying to put trait objects in a `List`.

name: mojo-serialization-ci-crash
trigger_phrases: Use when Mojo serialization test crashes with `mojo: error: execution crashed` and stack trace from `libKGENCompilerRTShared.so`, or CI shows exit code 1 on `test_serialization.mojo`.

name: mojo-set-api-migration-syntax-patterns
trigger_phrases: Use when Mojo compiler reports 'expected ) in call argument list' after a set() migration, or 'use of unknown declaration' errors appear after commenting out var declarations.

name: mojo-shallow-copy-tensor-hazard
trigger_phrases: Use when a function receives a tensor argument, makes a 'copy', mutates the copy, and the caller sees unexpected value changes, or numerical gradient checks produce wrong results despite correct mathematical derivations.

name: mojo-shape-noncontiguous-value-tests
trigger_phrases: Use when adding value-correctness tests for shape ops (reshape, flatten, permute, concatenate, tile, repeat, broadcast_to) on non-contiguous inputs, surfacing flat-index bugs where _get_float64(i) ignores _strides.

name: mojo-shared-test-helper-extraction
trigger_phrases: Use when a `fn` helper defined locally in a `test_*.mojo` file is (or will be) copied into sibling files, or a test file is being split and the helper must go somewhere.

name: mojo-signed-int-bitwise-not
trigger_phrases: Use when adding `~` operator tests for any signed Mojo integer type, following up on unsigned bitwise NOT tests, or creating new integer operator test files that must respect the per-file test count limit.

name: mojo-silent-noop-int8-dtype-fix
trigger_phrases: Use when a test contains a comment like 'silent no-op' or 'TODO: add int8 branch' for a setter function, or a dtype round-trip test uses `_set_int64` as a workaround because `_set_float64` doesn't work for that dtype.

name: mojo-simd-optimize
trigger_phrases: Use when optimizing tensor operations, vectorizing element-wise computations, working with performance-critical loops (>1000 elements), or benchmark results show optimization potential.

name: mojo-slice-negative-step
trigger_phrases: Use when a Mojo tensor/array `__getitem__(Slice)` returns 0 elements for `t[::-1]`, reverse-sliced result has wrong values, or a test for negative-step slicing is skipped.

name: mojo-stride-manipulation-for-noncontiguous-tests
trigger_phrases: Use when placeholder tests comment out `transpose(a)` with `# TODO: implement transpose()`, or you need to verify `is_contiguous()` returns `False` for non-standard stride patterns.

name: mojo-subpackage-import-tests
trigger_phrases: Use when a GitHub issue asks to verify a specific sub-module import path (e.g. `from pkg.sub import X`), or an existing `test_imports.mojo` file only tests parent-package imports but not the direct sub-package path.

name: mojo-tensor-multidim-setitem
trigger_phrases: Use when adding multi-dim mutable indexing (`t[[i, j]] = val`) to any Mojo tensor/array struct, or struct stores elements in a flat `UnsafePointer` buffer with a `_strides: List[Int]` field.

name: mojo-trait-conformance-fix
trigger_phrases: Use when a Mojo struct has `__hash__`, `__eq__`, `__lt__`, or other trait methods implemented but the trait (`Hashable`, `Comparable`, etc.) is missing from `struct Foo(...):`.

name: mojo-trait-conformance-for-sequential-integration
trigger_phrases: Use when you need to use existing Mojo layer structs inside `Sequential2`/`Sequential3` containers, or a layer struct is declared `(Copyable, Movable)` but not `Module`.

name: mojo-transient-crash-rerun
trigger_phrases: Use when CI fails with `mojo: error: execution crashed` (not an assertion error or compilation error), the crash log shows no stack frames from your repo's code — only from Mojo runtime libraries, and the PR contains no logic changes.

name: mojo-transpose-view-contiguity
trigger_phrases: Use when tests simulate non-contiguous tensors by directly mutating `_strides` field, or you need to test `is_contiguous()` returns `False` for a realistically transposed tensor.

name: mojo-tuple-destructor-clone-pattern
trigger_phrases: Use when ASAN reports `N bytes in M allocations of pooled_alloc` where M matches the number of datasets created in the test, or `Dataset.__getitem__` returns a `Tuple[AnyTensor, AnyTensor]` that wraps `.slice()` views.

name: mojo-tuple-return-types
trigger_phrases: Use when writing helpers that return multiple computed values, seeing 'no matching function in initialization' on tuple return types, or extracting shared logic that needs to return (start, end, step, size) style tuples.

name: mojo-type-alias-cleanup
trigger_phrases: Use when CI fails with 'unknown type' errors in Mojo after a PR removes deprecated `comptime` type aliases.

name: mojo-type-safety
trigger_phrases: Use when type errors occur during compilation, performing code review for type safety, designing generic functions, or working with trait constraints.

name: mojo-unsafepointer-bitcast-fix
trigger_phrases: Use when CI `Mojo Package Compilation` job fails with `error: 'UnsafePointer[?, ?, address_space=?]' value has no attribute 'address_of'`, or code needs to read the IEEE 754 bit representation of a float.

name: mojo-unsigned-wrapping-tests
trigger_phrases: Use when adding boundary/overflow tests to `test_unsigned.mojo` or similar Mojo unsigned type test files, or verifying `UInt8`/`UInt16`/`UInt32`/`UInt64` wrap-around behavior explicitly.

name: mojo-upstream-bug-filing-reproducibility-standard
trigger_phrases: Use before filing any Mojo bug report or feature request against `modular/modular`, or when writing a blog post or ADR claiming a Mojo feature is broken or unsupported.

name: mojo-val-accuracy-behavioral-test
trigger_phrases: Use when a GH issue flags that only default values are tested but not behavioral effects, or when closing a test gap between init-defaults tests and run-effects tests.

name: mojo-validation-loop-accuracy-tracking
trigger_phrases: Use when a `ValidationLoop.run()` (or equivalent) calls `metrics.update_val_metrics(loss, 0.0)` with a hardcoded accuracy placeholder, or a GitHub issue asks for a follow-up test proving `metrics.val_accuracy` is updated after `run()`.

name: mojo-value-verification-tests
trigger_phrases: Use when a test calls `assert_numel` and `assert_dim` but never checks what values are in the tensor, or implementing follow-up issue to add value verification to shape operation tests.

name: mojo-version-migration-strategy
trigger_phrases: Use when planning a Mojo version upgrade on a codebase with >50 `.mojo` files, coordinating multiple agents working on the same migration, or deciding which files to fix first when errors cascade across dependencies.

name: mojo-view-semantics-test
trigger_phrases: Use when a `slice()` method docstring claims view semantics but no test mutates through the slice, or adding regression coverage to catch copy-vs-view regressions in tensor ops.

name: mojo-writable-write-to-migration
trigger_phrases: Use when auditing a Mojo 0.26.3+ codebase for deprecated `Stringable`/`__str__` patterns, or a struct declares `Writable` in its trait list but implements `__str__` instead of `write_to`.

name: move-loose-test-files
trigger_phrases: Use when a quality audit identifies `.py` test files at `tests/unit/` root (excluding `__init__.py`, `conftest.py`), or a new source sub-package `scylla/X/` exists but its tests landed in `tests/unit/` root.

name: multi-division-fixture-management
trigger_phrases: Use when you have a flat `tests/fixtures/*.json` layout and need to add a second fixture set, your `capture_fixtures` CLI writes to a hardcoded output directory, or integration tests reference a single `FIXTURES_DIR` constant.

name: multi-domain-audit-remediation
trigger_phrases: Use when an audit scorecard surfaces findings across exception handling, logging, DRY, CI/CD, and docs simultaneously and you need to work through them in a safe order.

name: multi-experiment-figure-pipeline
trigger_phrases: Use when adding new Altair figures with data-dependent guards, creating task-level analysis, or scaling figure pipelines to large experiment counts.

name: multi-judge-consensus
trigger_phrases: Use this skill when you need to add multi-judge support to any evaluation system that currently uses a single judge, implement consensus voting across multiple LLM evaluators.

name: multi-language-judge-pipelines
trigger_phrases: Use when test infrastructure needs multi-language support for E2E or judge systems that evaluate code in multiple programming languages, or different languages require different tooling.

name: multi-pattern-stale-detection
trigger_phrases: Use when you have a CI matrix validator that calls `pattern.split()` to expand multiple patterns, or a CI group references multiple test files via a space-separated pattern string.

name: multi-pr-issue-triage
trigger_phrases: Use when facing a backlog of related GitHub issues with mixed resolution status, some issues are already resolved by merged PRs, or some issues are interdependent.

name: multidim-slice-step-validation
trigger_phrases: Use when a variadic-arg `__getitem__(*slices)` overload computes `start`/`end` from each slice but never reads `step`, so `tensor[::2, :]` silently returns every element.

name: multiprocess-to-multithread-conversion
trigger_phrases: Use when Python 3.14t (free-threaded) `ProcessPoolExecutor` hangs silently on `pool.submit()`, or workers primarily run external CLI subprocesses via `subprocess.run()`/`Popen()`.

name: mypy-annotate-test-functions
headings_h2: ## Key Insight: Most Tests May Already Be Annotated
## Key Insight: Helper Classes in Test Files Need Annotations Too
## Key Insight: `object` vs `Any` for Ignored Optional Parameters
trigger_phrases: Use when an issue asks to 'annotate test functions in tests/unit/X/ for mypy compliance', or you need to add `-> None` to all `def test_*()` methods in pytest test classes.

name: myrmidon-container-execution-volume-mapping
trigger_phrases: Use when the claude-myrmidon NATS pipeline worker calls `subprocess.run(["claude", "-p", ...])` directly on the host, or any agent invocation that runs `claude` CLI without container isolation.

name: myrmidon-swarm-end-to-end-orchestration-full-workflow
trigger_phrases: Use when end-to-end task spans 3+ distinct phases with dependencies between phases, mix of destructive operations (worktree removal, force-push) and creative operations (PR creation, knowledge capture).
